text: Here we present hybrid transanal and robotic TME. Fifty-seven years old male patient was diagnosed with advanced rectal cancer. He underwent chemoradiotherapy and was settled for operation.

The surgeon begins transanal total mesorectal excision (TME) with a Lone Star retractor in place. The tumor is located two centimeters above the dentate line. The rectal wall is incised circumferentially at the dentate line, and a purse-string suture is placed on the proximal part of the rectum. Dissection starts from the posterior part of the rectum.

The robotic total mesorectal excision (TME) is performed, beginning with high ligation of the inferior mesenteric artery (IMA) and vein (IMV). The surgeon dissects the posterior side, followed by the anterior and lateral parts of the mesorectum.
The surgeon connects the transanal and robotic TME dissection planes, removes the specimen through a five-centimeter abdominal incision, and performs a side-to-end coloanal anastomosis.
Finally, astrology showed YPT, three and zero. Thank you.
